prompt:
Vertical 9:16 cinematic devotional oil painting video clip, slower pacing with longer holds and restrained cuts.

Classical devotional oil painting realism. atmosphere of a body hung and pulled by its own weight; visible brushwork, craquelure, aged varnish throughout.

Scene contains:
one arm and shoulder of the crucified man seen close, the arm stretched rigid and straight to the nailed hand at the beam, the shoulder joint pulled and distended by the dead weight of the hanging body, the tendons drawn, the skin taut over strained muscle, deep shadow beyond. The scene is a painted tableau — a frozen moment, not an action. Nothing moves; this is a still painting.

Scene:
Start on full composition — the whole painted scene.

Cut to mid framing — the dominant subject of the painting.

Cut to close-up — the rigid stretched arm.

Cut to insert — the distended shoulder joint.

Cut to macro detail — the nailed hand at the beam.

Cut to lower framing — the drawn tendons.

Return to full composition.

End hold on the rigid stretched arm.

Micro-motion:
- minimal

Camera:
- crop-based reframing only
- no invented movement

Emotion:
a body hung and pulled by its own weight

No added elements.

10.0s

9:16
negative_prompt: no camera invention, no zoom invented, no pan invented, no push-in invented, no dolly, no tracking, no rotation, no animated subject motion, no figures moving, no morphing, no scene morphing, no facial morphing, no glow added, no light rays added beyond what is painted, no atmospheric haze, no scene enhancement, no divine effects added, no smooth transitions, no motion blur, no reinterpretation, no new elements, no text, no watermarks, no extra limbs, no flickering, no warping, no animation, no action, no subject performs any task, no body motion, no limbs moving, the painting is fixed and frozen, only the camera reframes within the still image

beats.description: Start on full composition — the whole painted scene.
Cut to mid framing — the dominant subject of the painting.
Cut to close-up — the rigid stretched arm.
Cut to insert — the distended shoulder joint.
Cut to macro detail — the nailed hand at the beam.
Cut to lower framing — the drawn tendons.
End hold on the rigid stretched arm.
